entry.title: Text in GOS note popup not cleared after calling cl_gos_document_service->edit_note
entry.content:
I have implemented logic in a custom report to call the GOS popup for a specific note associated with a Business Partner in the customer role. The report displays a list of customers, with hotspots on each line item that allow the user to open the corresponding note.

If the customer does not yet have a note, the report calls cl_gos_document_service->create_note. If the note already exists, the report calls  cl_gos_document_service->edit_note.

The notes are successfully created and updated using these methods. However, an issue occurs after calling  cl_gos_document_service->edit_note: the previous text is not cleared from the popup. The text loaded during the first call to  cl_gos_document_service->edit_note continues to appear in all subsequent calls to both create_note and edit_note.

Does anyone know of a way to prevent this?

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Version Restrictions
entry.content:
Dear @FranConde,

In Public Cloud the SAP-recommended way is not to hide a consolidation version by catalogs but to control it via role restrictions / restriction types on the authorization field for Consolidation Version

In Public Cloud you maintain these checks via Business Roles + Restrictions in Maintain Business Roles and you use Display Restriction Types to see which catalogs support which restriction entities

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/9203905781b441ed9359cb29803f000a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/8980ad05330b4585ab96a8e09cef4688.html

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/defining-restrictions-for-business-roles

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/c926d691d7144f7dba16f8e12ad81d28.html

Best-practice approach:

1. Start from SAP delivered Group Reporting role templates

SAP delivers Group Reporting templates/roles such as:

  • SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT_GRP, SAP_BR_GRP_ACCOUNTANT, SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR_GRP, SAP_BR_EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_GRP, SAP_BR_BUSINESS_ANALYST_GRP

These come with the Group Reporting scope content, SAP notes 1SG scope activation as a prerequisite for templates in some tenants

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3620561

Recommendation is to copy the needed templates to Z roles then maintain restrictions there upgrade-safe pattern

2. Restrict

In Maintain Business Roles:

a. Open the role

b. Go to Maintain Restrictions

c. For the Group Reporting catalogs in that role find the restriction type for:

    • Consolidation Version

d. Set access to Restricted and maintain allowed values so that A10 is NOT included

    • For example allow A00/A01/… but exclude A10

Do this for all Group Reporting roles that should not access A10 (Group Accountant, Analyst, etc.)

3. Don’t miss a catalog:

Use Display Restriction Types to:

  • search for RVERS / Consolidation Version
  • see which business catalogs use it
  • then confirm you maintained the restriction for those catalogs in the roles

This is important because users can otherwise still access A10 via another catalog/app path especially reporting vs processing

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/9203905781b441ed9359cb29803f000a.html

4. Validate with Display Authorization Trace

If a test user can still select/run A10 somewhere use Display Authorization Trace app to see which check/catalog granted it Public Cloud equivalent of troubleshooting missing/excess authorizations

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/b8c8128b86ae4e90b0ebb582d27a1980.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite- Deep dive into Content Modifier
entry.content:
It's all in the error message

Method with name: replaceAll('X','') not found on bean: org.apache.camel.converter.stream.InputStreamCache

This means that the message body is treated as InputStreamCache and not java.lang.String when using Expressions in the Body section of a Content Modifier. Couple ways around this:

1. Use the properties tab, which uses String type by default.

/* Property Tab - Set to property, use the property in Body tab */
${body.replaceAll('X','')}

2. Skip the extra Property entirely and just cast the body to a String via bodyAs(<class>). This works in the Body/Property/Headers tabs, though I wouldn't do it as a header due to size limitations in headers.

${bodyAs(java.lang.String).replaceAll('X','')}

entry.title: Re: Issue with Deferred Revenue Posting in SAP ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello Gloria, 

A down payment received should not be revenue - it is just an advance payment from the customer.  First you must, from the sales order ideally, create the Down Payment Request and you can send it to the customer.  No journal entries are created at this point.   When the customer pays the Down Payment, and the cash is posted against the Down Payment Request, then there is a JE which debits cash, and credits the Accounts Receivable account that has been designated in the ADG determination activity.  The cash from the customer is not (nor should it be) deferred revenue.  When you create the subsequent customer invoice, the system will see the down payment and offer to net it against the invoice.   When you do a revenue recognition run, it looks at the rules you set up in Accrual Method Determination without regard to the existence of any down payment. 

Thanks, 

Carol Casey

Phoenix Business Consulting

entry.title: State central bank indicator in vendor master data?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is there (in SAP S/4HANA 2023) any technical way to get the SCB indicator into a vendor's master in order to use it the same way we use payment terms or payment methods etc.? 

I stumbled across this with business partner master data, but there is no trace of an SCB indicator in the vendor master record in the company code (LFB1), but only in the document item (BSEG). From a purely functional point of view, however, this would be a great solution, as we usually always use the same SCB indicator for the same supplier. It would be great if it were then automatically transferred from the master record to the invoice documents.

Is this supported in any way?

Thank You and best regards

Rado

entry.title: Re: How to download WBS element or WBS element overview r...
entry.content:
Hi Chris,

Have you tested the functionality yourself before posting your response on the forum? If so, please share screenshots to support the points you mentioned. Firstly, the Fiori app “Project Control – Enterprise Projects” is not designed to display the project together with its WBS elements. This app only provides high‑level project details. In order to see full project hierarchy with associated WBS elements you should use the fiori App "Project Planning".  Could you please provide a screenshot of the Project Control – Enterprise Projects app showing a project together with its WBS elements, as you described?
